Beej's Bit Bucket

 ⚡ Tech and Programming Fun

2026-03-12

On Making

TLDR: I gain a lot of fulfillment by making things. I don't consider things built by others at my request to be made by me, and are therefore much less fulfilling.

There have been a lot of pieces written about what I'll call “the AI dev schism”. I think there's a lot of truth to those discussions, but there's something else that troubles me.

I Am Uncomfortable

I don't like lying. Yes, all that has been created and I was the initiator of that creation. But I don't really feel like I made any of it. I'm uncomfortable claiming that I did so.

Having others complete projects I initiated is entirely less fulfilling to me. It's not just the loss of the craft and the problem-solving challenge. It's the loss of making.

What Did I Make Recently?

My wife wanted a no-frills flash card system for learning Spanish, so I wrote it by hand. I can put my name on that code and say that I made it.
